skill.name: researching-best-practices
skill.description: Researches current software engineering best practices and turns them into context-specific recommendations.
skill.summary: Find current best practices, compare approaches, and recommend practical software engineering decisions.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are documentation false positives in markdown checklists and examples. Backticks, route examples, security header names, and checklist terms were misread as executable commands, weak cryptography, storage access, or reconnaissance. The only real risk factor is declared web research capability through WebSearch and WebFetch, which is expected for this skill.

low_findings.title: Declared Web Research Capability
low_findings.description: The skill declares WebSearch and WebFetch and instructs web research when needed. This is a legitimate capability for best-practice research, but marketplace users should know external sources may be consulted.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools metadata and methodology explicitly permit web research. The behavior matches the skill purpose and does not show exfiltration intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misidentified as Execution
low_findings.description: Static command findings are markdown examples and documentation snippets, including code fences and route examples. No executable script files are present in the package, and the skill does not declare a shell tool.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside markdown documentation or code fences. There is no packaged executable code path or allowed shell execution tool in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Security Checklist Terms Misidentified as Sensitive Access
low_findings.description: Findings for weak cryptography, browser storage, Windows SAM, and reconnaissance are checklist guidance or security header text. They describe practices to check, not behavior performed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are checklist items and HTTP header names. The Windows SAM hit maps to SAMEORIGIN in X-Frame-Options, not database access.

low_findings.title: Missing Referenced Helper Files
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references scripts, assets, and some reference names that are not present in the file tree. This is a quality and reliability issue, not a confirmed security threat.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file tree contains only README.md, SKILL.md, and four reference checklists. The missing paths may confuse users but do not create direct execution risk.

content.value_statement: Software teams need current guidance that fits their codebase and constraints. This skill researches standards, compares options, and explains recommended approaches clearly.
actual_capabilities: Researches current software engineering standards using local references and web sources.
Compares implementation approaches with trade-offs and context fit.
Reviews existing code patterns with read-only codebase inspection tools.
Provides guidance for security, performance, testing, API design, and code quality.
Structures recommendations with rationale, alternatives, pitfalls, and migration steps.
Uses included checklist references for API design, code quality, performance, and security.
limitations: Does not implement changes or edit project files by itself.
Depends on source quality when using web research or community references.
May require project context to avoid generic recommendations.
References some script and asset paths that are not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Choose an Architecture Approach
use_cases.description: Compare architecture options against scale, team experience, delivery timeline, and maintainability needs.

use_cases.title: Review Code Practices
use_cases.description: Assess error handling, validation, testing, and structure against established best-practice checklists.

use_cases.title: Prepare Technical Recommendations
use_cases.description: Turn a broad implementation question into a researched recommendation with alternatives and trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Ask for a Best Practice
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the best way to handle authentication for my web app? Explain the main options and recommend one.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Approaches
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare server-side sessions and HTTP-only cookie JWT authentication for a React app with a Node API.

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Patterns
prompt_templates.scenario: Use project context before recommending changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current error handling approach and tell me whether it follows best practices for this codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Decision Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced recommendation for a technical decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research API versioning strategies for our public API. Provide a recommendation, alternatives, risks, and a migration plan.

output_examples.input: What is the best way to design pagination for a REST API?
output_examples.output: A recommendation that compares offset and cursor pagination, explains trade-offs, and suggests cursor pagination for large changing datasets.

output_examples.input: Is my React state management approach still appropriate?
output_examples.output: A context review that checks current project patterns, identifies when local state is enough, and lists cases that justify a shared state library.

output_examples.input: How should we improve our security checklist before launch?
output_examples.output: A prioritized checklist covering authentication, authorization, input validation, headers, dependency scanning, logging, and release gates.

best_practices: Provide project context, technology versions, constraints, and decision goals before asking for recommendations.
Ask for trade-offs and alternatives when the decision affects architecture, security, or long-term maintenance.
Request citations or source summaries when recommendations rely on current standards or external research.
anti_patterns: Using generic best practices without checking existing codebase conventions.
Treating the first recommendation as final without considering constraints and failure modes.
Requesting implementation advice without describing security, performance, or operational requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill change my code?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for research and recommendation workflows, not direct file editing.

faq.question: Can it use current sources?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill declares WebSearch and WebFetch so it can research current standards when needed.

faq.question: What topics does it cover best?
faq.answer: It covers architecture, API design, code quality, security, performance, testing, and tool selection.

faq.question: Does it replace expert review?
faq.answer: No. Use it to prepare decisions and checklists, then validate high-impact changes with responsible owners.

faq.question: Can it review my existing project patterns?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use read-only code inspection tools to understand conventions before recommending changes.

faq.question: Why are some referenced resources unavailable?
faq.answer: The package includes several checklist files, but SKILL.md also mentions scripts and templates that are not included.
